metadata.description: Dinobase benchmark: 75 questions across 5 verticals, 3 tiers each

tiers.2: Multi-step within one source (joins, window functions, CTEs)

revops.description: Revenue Operations: CRM pipeline + billing data for a B2B SaaS company

questions.question: Give me a quick snapshot of our sales pipeline — how many deals are we working?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS answer FROM hubspot.deals
questions.explanation: Simple count of all deals in the CRM.

questions.question: What is our active subscriber count right now? I need it for the weekly metrics email.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS answer FROM stripe.subscriptions WHERE status = 'active'
questions.explanation: Simple filtered count on subscription status.

questions.question: What's our total closed-won deal value? The board wants the number for the investor deck.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT SUM(amount) AS answer FROM hubspot.deals WHERE dealstage = 'closedwon'
questions.explanation: Sum of deal amounts filtered to closed-won stage. HubSpot amounts are already in dollars.

questions.question: Where are most of our deals sitting in the pipeline right now?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT dealstage AS answer, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM hubspot.deals GROUP BY dealstage ORDER BY cnt DESC LIMIT 1
questions.expected_answer: closedwon
questions.explanation: Group by deal stage and find the mode.

questions.question: I think we have a payment processing issue — how many charges are failing?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS answer FROM stripe.charges WHERE status = 'failed'
questions.explanation: Simple filtered count on charge status.

questions.question: I need our MRR number for the investor update — what are we at?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN plan_interval = 'month' THEN plan_amount ELSE plan_amount / 12.0 END) / 100.0, 2) AS answer FROM stripe.subscriptions WHERE status = 'active'
questions.explanation: MRR requires converting annual plans to monthly (divide by 12) and converting cents to dollars (divide by 100). Both transformations are needed.

questions.question: The CEO is asking about our win rate — what percentage of deals are we actually closing?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN dealstage = 'closedwon' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / SUM(CASE WHEN dealstage IN ('closedwon', 'closedlost') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 1) AS answer FROM hubspot.deals
questions.explanation: Win rate = closedwon / (closedwon + closedlost). Must NOT include open/other stages in denominator.

questions.question: Break down our average deal size by stage — I want to see where the big deals are landing.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT dealstage, ROUND(AVG(amount), 2) AS avg_amount FROM hubspot.deals GROUP BY dealstage ORDER BY avg_amount DESC LIMIT 3
questions.expected_answer: top 3 stages by avg deal size
questions.explanation: Aggregation with GROUP BY and ordering. Tests ability to rank stages.

questions.question: Which customers are in real trouble — delinquent AND failing payments? I want to flag them for the collections team.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS answer FROM stripe.customers c JOIN stripe.charges ch ON c.id = ch.customer_id WHERE c.delinquent = true AND ch.status = 'failed'
questions.explanation: Join within Stripe source between customers and charges, filtering on both tables.

questions.question: Finance needs to know how much revenue we have actually collected from paid invoices, in real dollars.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT ROUND(SUM(amount_paid) / 100.0, 2) AS answer FROM stripe.invoices WHERE status = 'paid'
questions.explanation: Must filter to paid invoices and convert cents to dollars.

questions.question: I think some won deals never converted to paying customers — can you find companies where we closed the deal but they have no active subscription?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT DISTINCT co.name AS answer FROM hubspot.deals d JOIN hubspot.contacts ct ON d.contact_id = ct.id JOIN hubspot.companies co ON ct.company_id = co.id WHERE d.dealstage = 'closedwon' AND ct.email NOT IN (SELECT c.email FROM stripe.customers c JOIN stripe.subscriptions s ON c.id = s.customer_id WHERE s.status = 'active')
questions.expected_answer: none — all companies with closed-won deals have active subscriptions
questions.explanation: Requires joining HubSpot deals->contacts->companies then anti-joining against Stripe subscriptions via email. Finds revenue leakage.

questions.question: How much MRR are we getting from contacts that HubSpot considers actual customers? I want to validate our CRM lifecycle stages.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN s.plan_interval = 'month' THEN s.plan_amount ELSE s.plan_amount / 12.0 END) / 100.0, 2) AS answer FROM hubspot.contacts hc JOIN stripe.customers sc ON hc.email = sc.email JOIN stripe.subscriptions s ON sc.id = s.customer_id WHERE hc.lifecyclestage = 'customer' AND s.status = 'active'
questions.explanation: Cross-source join via email. Must convert cents to dollars and normalize annual to monthly.

questions.question: I am prepping for the QBR — can you show me how subscription value correlates with deal stage? Like, what do people at each stage actually pay us?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT d.dealstage, ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN s.plan_interval = 'month' THEN s.plan_amount ELSE s.plan_amount / 12.0 END) / 100.0, 2) AS avg_monthly_dollars FROM hubspot.deals d JOIN hubspot.contacts hc ON d.contact_id = hc.id JOIN stripe.customers sc ON hc.email = sc.email JOIN stripe.subscriptions s ON sc.id = s.customer_id WHERE s.status = 'active' GROUP BY d.dealstage ORDER BY avg_monthly_dollars DESC
questions.expected_answer: table of deal stages with avg subscription amounts
questions.explanation: Complex cross-source join: deals->contacts->stripe customers->subscriptions. Requires cents conversion and annual normalization.

questions.question: Which industry vertical is bringing in the most actual revenue? Not pipeline — real charges collected.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT co.industry AS answer, ROUND(SUM(ch.amount) / 100.0, 2) AS total_revenue FROM hubspot.contacts hc JOIN hubspot.companies co ON hc.company_id = co.id JOIN stripe.customers sc ON hc.email = sc.email JOIN stripe.charges ch ON sc.id = ch.customer_id WHERE ch.status = 'succeeded' GROUP BY co.industry ORDER BY total_revenue DESC LIMIT 1
questions.expected_answer: top industry name
questions.explanation: Multi-hop cross-source join: contacts->companies for industry, contacts->stripe customers->charges for revenue.

questions.question: Pull me a report of our top 5 highest-spending contacts with their CRM details — lifecycle stage, company, the works. For the account review meeting.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT hc.firstname || ' ' || hc.lastname AS contact_name, hc.lifecyclestage, co.name AS company_name, ROUND(SUM(ch.amount) / 100.0, 2) AS total_charges_dollars FROM hubspot.contacts hc JOIN hubspot.companies co ON hc.company_id = co.id JOIN stripe.customers sc ON hc.email = sc.email JOIN stripe.charges ch ON sc.id = ch.customer_id WHERE ch.status = 'succeeded' GROUP BY hc.firstname, hc.lastname, hc.lifecyclestage, co.name ORDER BY total_charges_dollars DESC LIMIT 5
questions.expected_answer: ranked list of top 5 contacts with charges
questions.explanation: Full cross-source join enriching Stripe billing data with HubSpot CRM context.

ecommerce.description: E-commerce analytics: Shopify storefront + Stripe payment processing

questions.question: Quick check — how many orders have come through the store?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS answer FROM shopify.orders
questions.explanation: Simple count of all orders.

questions.question: What is our AOV looking like? I need it for the marketing deck.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(total_price), 2) AS answer FROM shopify.orders
questions.explanation: Simple average on Shopify orders. Prices already in dollars.

questions.question: What do we actually have live on the storefront right now? I want the active product count.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS answer FROM shopify.products WHERE status = 'active'
questions.explanation: Simple filtered count on product status.

questions.question: Are we having a refund problem? How many orders have been refunded?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS answer FROM shopify.orders WHERE financial_status = 'refunded'
questions.explanation: Simple filtered count. Refund rate is ~4.3% of 415 orders.

questions.question: How big is our product catalog in total, including stuff that is not active?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS answer FROM shopify.products
questions.explanation: Simple unfiltered count of products.

questions.question: What is our refund rate? I want to know if it is getting out of hand.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN financial_status IN ('refunded', 'partially_refunded') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 1) AS answer FROM shopify.orders
questions.explanation: Must include both 'refunded' and 'partially_refunded' in numerator.

questions.question: Which product categories are actually driving revenue? Give me the top 5.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT p.product_type, ROUND(SUM(o.total_price), 2) AS total_revenue FROM shopify.orders o JOIN shopify.products p ON o.product_id = p.id WHERE o.financial_status = 'paid' GROUP BY p.product_type ORDER BY total_revenue DESC LIMIT 5
questions.expected_answer: ranked list of product types
questions.explanation: Join orders to products within Shopify, aggregate by product type.

questions.question: How much have we actually processed through Stripe? I need the total in dollars, not cents.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT ROUND(SUM(amount) / 100.0, 2) AS answer FROM stripe_ecom.charges WHERE status = 'succeeded'
questions.explanation: Stripe amounts are in cents. Must divide by 100 to get dollars.

questions.question: Which vendor commands the highest price point on average? Trying to figure out our premium tier.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT vendor AS answer, ROUND(AVG(price), 2) AS avg_price FROM shopify.products WHERE status = 'active' GROUP BY vendor ORDER BY avg_price DESC LIMIT 1
questions.expected_answer: vendor name
questions.explanation: Group by vendor, compute average price, rank.

questions.question: We have a fulfillment backlog — what percentage of orders are still unfulfilled?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN fulfillment_status = 'unfulfilled' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 1) AS answer FROM shopify.orders
questions.explanation: Percentage calculation on fulfillment status.

questions.question: Finance flagged a discrepancy between what Shopify says we sold and what Stripe actually charged. Can you reconcile the numbers for customers in both systems?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT ROUND(ABS(SUM(o.total_price) - SUM(DISTINCT ch.amount) / 100.0), 2) AS discrepancy_dollars FROM shopify.customers sc JOIN shopify.orders o ON sc.email = o.email JOIN stripe_ecom.customers stc ON sc.email = stc.email JOIN stripe_ecom.charges ch ON stc.id = ch.customer_id WHERE o.financial_status = 'paid' AND ch.status = 'succeeded'
questions.explanation: Cross-source revenue reconciliation. Stripe is in cents, Shopify in dollars. The discrepancy reveals payment processing differences.

questions.question: We are losing customers somewhere in the funnel — who placed Shopify orders but never got charged in Stripe?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT DISTINCT sc.email AS answer FROM shopify.customers sc JOIN shopify.orders o ON sc.email = o.email WHERE sc.email NOT IN (SELECT stc.email FROM stripe_ecom.customers stc JOIN stripe_ecom.charges ch ON stc.id = ch.customer_id)
questions.expected_answer: list of customer emails
questions.explanation: Anti-join pattern across sources to find customers missing from Stripe.

questions.question: Take our top 10 customers by order volume and tell me what they have actually been charged in Stripe. I want to see if the numbers line up.
questions.ground_truth_sql: WITH top_shopify AS (SELECT sc.email, sc.orders_count FROM shopify.customers sc ORDER BY sc.orders_count DESC LIMIT 10) SELECT ts.email, ts.orders_count, ROUND(SUM(ch.amount) / 100.0, 2) AS stripe_total_dollars FROM top_shopify ts JOIN stripe_ecom.customers stc ON ts.email = stc.email JOIN stripe_ecom.charges ch ON stc.id = ch.customer_id WHERE ch.status = 'succeeded' GROUP BY ts.email, ts.orders_count ORDER BY ts.orders_count DESC
questions.expected_answer: table of top 10 customers with Shopify orders and Stripe charges
questions.explanation: CTE to find top Shopify customers, then cross-source join to Stripe for charge data. Cents-to-dollars conversion required.

questions.question: What does the average Stripe charge look like per Shopify order? I am trying to understand our effective take rate.
questions.ground_truth_sql: WITH shopify_totals AS (SELECT o.email, COUNT(*) AS order_count FROM shopify.orders o WHERE o.financial_status = 'paid' GROUP BY o.email), stripe_totals AS (SELECT stc.email, SUM(ch.amount) / 100.0 AS total_charges_dollars FROM stripe_ecom.customers stc JOIN stripe_ecom.charges ch ON stc.id = ch.customer_id WHERE ch.status = 'succeeded' GROUP BY stc.email) SELECT ROUND(AVG(st.total_charges_dollars / sht.order_count), 2) AS answer FROM shopify_totals sht JOIN stripe_totals st ON sht.email = st.email
questions.explanation: Cross-source ratio: Stripe charge dollars per Shopify order. Requires aggregating both sides then joining.

questions.question: Which product categories are causing the most payment refunds? I need to figure out if we have a quality issue in a specific category.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT p.product_type, ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN ch.status = 'refunded' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 1) AS refund_rate FROM shopify.orders o JOIN shopify.products p ON o.product_id = p.id JOIN stripe_ecom.customers stc ON o.email = stc.email JOIN stripe_ecom.charges ch ON stc.id = ch.customer_id GROUP BY p.product_type ORDER BY refund_rate DESC LIMIT 5
questions.expected_answer: ranked list of product types by Stripe refund rate
questions.explanation: Three-way cross-source join: Shopify orders->products for product type, then to Stripe charges for refund status.

knowledge_base.description: Knowledge management: Notion wiki + GitHub issues + Slack discussions

questions.question: How much of our Notion wiki is actually published and available to the team?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS answer FROM notion.pages WHERE status = 'published'
questions.explanation: Simple filtered count on page status.

questions.question: Give me a health check on our issue backlog — how many open GitHub issues do we have?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS answer FROM github.issues WHERE state = 'open'
questions.explanation: Simple filtered count. NOTE: Do NOT use github.repos.open_issues_count as it is stale.

questions.question: How active is Slack? I want to know how many top-level conversations are happening, not just thread noise.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS answer FROM slack.messages WHERE thread_ts IS NULL
questions.explanation: thread_ts IS NULL means top-level message. NOT NULL means thread reply.

questions.question: Quick one — what is our total repo count?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS answer FROM github.repos
questions.explanation: Simple count of all repos.

questions.question: Trying to understand our knowledge base structure — what is the total Notion database count?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS answer FROM notion.databases
questions.explanation: Simple count of Notion databases.

questions.question: Which Slack channels have the most engaged discussions? I want to see where the real threaded conversations are happening.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.name AS channel_name, COUNT(*) AS thread_replies FROM slack.messages m JOIN slack.channels c ON m.channel_id = c.id WHERE m.thread_ts IS NOT NULL GROUP BY c.name ORDER BY thread_replies DESC LIMIT 5
questions.expected_answer: ranked list of channels by thread activity
questions.explanation: Join messages to channels, filter to thread replies (thread_ts NOT NULL), aggregate.

questions.question: How fast are we closing issues on average? I need the cycle time for the engineering retrospective.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (closed_at::TIMESTAMP - created_at::TIMESTAMP)) / 86400.0), 1) AS answer FROM github.issues WHERE state = 'closed' AND closed_at IS NOT NULL
questions.explanation: Time difference between created_at and closed_at for closed issues.

questions.question: Which repos are creating the most backlog pain? Show me where open issues are piling up — and do not use the stale counter on the repo, count from actual issues.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT r.name, COUNT(*) AS open_issues FROM github.issues i JOIN github.repos r ON i.repo_id = r.id WHERE i.state = 'open' GROUP BY r.name ORDER BY open_issues DESC LIMIT 5
questions.expected_answer: ranked list of repos by open issue count
questions.explanation: Must count from issues table, NOT use repos.open_issues_count which is stale.

questions.question: How much content have we actually written across our Notion wiki? Break it down by database so I can see which areas are well-documented.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT d.title AS database_name, SUM(p.word_count) AS total_words FROM notion.pages p JOIN notion.databases d ON p.parent_id = d.id WHERE p.status = 'published' GROUP BY d.title ORDER BY total_words DESC
questions.expected_answer: table of databases with word counts
questions.explanation: Join pages to databases, filter published, aggregate word count.

questions.question: Which programming language is getting the most community love in terms of stars?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT language AS answer, SUM(stargazers_count) AS total_stars FROM github.repos WHERE language IS NOT NULL GROUP BY language ORDER BY total_stars DESC LIMIT 1
questions.expected_answer: language name
questions.explanation: Aggregate stars by language across repos.

questions.question: Who on the team is truly cross-functional? I want to find people who are active across Notion, GitHub, AND Slack.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT n.created_by AS username FROM (SELECT DISTINCT created_by FROM notion.pages) n JOIN (SELECT DISTINCT author FROM github.issues) g ON n.created_by = g.author JOIN (SELECT DISTINCT user FROM slack.messages) s ON n.created_by = s.user ORDER BY username
questions.expected_answer: list of usernames active across all three platforms
questions.explanation: Three-way cross-source join on username to find users present in all systems.

questions.question: I am curious if people who write a lot of docs also file a lot of issues, or if they are different personas. Can you compare Notion output vs GitHub activity per person?
questions.ground_truth_sql: WITH notion_counts AS (SELECT created_by AS username, COUNT(*) AS page_count FROM notion.pages GROUP BY created_by), github_counts AS (SELECT author AS username, COUNT(*) AS issue_count FROM github.issues GROUP BY author) SELECT n.username, n.page_count, g.issue_count FROM notion_counts n JOIN github_counts g ON n.username = g.username ORDER BY n.page_count DESC
questions.expected_answer: table showing page count vs issue count per user
questions.explanation: Cross-source join on username to compare productivity across Notion and GitHub.

questions.question: We have some people who are super chatty in Slack but I wonder if they are actually shipping. Show me the top Slack posters and how many GitHub issues they have filed.
questions.ground_truth_sql: WITH slack_activity AS (SELECT user AS username, COUNT(*) AS message_count FROM slack.messages GROUP BY user ORDER BY message_count DESC LIMIT 5), github_activity AS (SELECT author AS username, COUNT(*) AS issue_count FROM github.issues GROUP BY author) SELECT sa.username, sa.message_count, COALESCE(ga.issue_count, 0) AS issue_count FROM slack_activity sa LEFT JOIN github_activity ga ON sa.username = ga.username ORDER BY sa.message_count DESC
questions.expected_answer: table of top 5 Slack users with their GitHub issue counts
questions.explanation: Cross-source comparison of Slack activity vs GitHub activity per user.

questions.question: For each of our repos, how much cross-platform buzz is there? I want to know which repos get talked about in Slack and documented in Notion.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT r.name AS repo_name, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM slack.messages m WHERE m.text_length > 0 AND m.channel_id IN (SELECT c.id FROM slack.channels c WHERE c.name LIKE '%' || r.name || '%')) AS slack_mentions, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM notion.pages p WHERE p.title LIKE '%' || r.name || '%') AS notion_pages FROM github.repos r ORDER BY repo_name
questions.expected_answer: table of repos with cross-platform reference counts
questions.explanation: Cross-source analysis using repo names to find mentions in Slack channels and Notion pages. Approximate due to text matching.

questions.question: Who are our most prolific contributors when you combine docs writing and issue filing? I need this for the eng productivity review.
questions.ground_truth_sql: WITH notion_counts AS (SELECT created_by AS username, COUNT(*) AS contributions FROM notion.pages GROUP BY created_by), github_counts AS (SELECT author AS username, COUNT(*) AS contributions FROM github.issues GROUP BY author), combined AS (SELECT COALESCE(n.username, g.username) AS username, COALESCE(n.contributions, 0) + COALESCE(g.contributions, 0) AS total_contributions FROM notion_counts n FULL OUTER JOIN github_counts g ON n.username = g.username) SELECT username, total_contributions FROM combined ORDER BY total_contributions DESC LIMIT 10
questions.expected_answer: ranked list of users by total contributions across Notion and GitHub
questions.explanation: Full outer join across sources to combine contribution counts. Uses COALESCE for users only in one system.

devops.description: DevOps and reliability: GitHub PRs/deployments + PagerDuty incidents + Datadog monitoring

questions.question: Can you pull together our merged PR stats for the engineering standup?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS answer FROM github.pull_requests WHERE state = 'merged'
questions.explanation: Simple filtered count. Must use state = 'merged', not just closed.

questions.question: Are we having deploy issues? Give me the failed deployment count.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS answer FROM github.deployments WHERE status = 'failure'
questions.explanation: Simple filtered count on deployment status.

questions.question: How is our incident response looking — how many PagerDuty incidents have we actually resolved?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS answer FROM pagerduty.incidents WHERE status = 'resolved'
questions.explanation: Simple filtered count on incident status.

questions.question: Anything on fire in monitoring right now? How many Datadog alerts are active?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS answer FROM datadog.monitors WHERE status = 'Alert'
questions.explanation: Simple filtered count on monitor status.

questions.question: Is oncall getting hammered? I need the high-urgency incident count.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS answer FROM pagerduty.incidents WHERE urgency = 'high'
questions.explanation: Simple filtered count on urgency field.

questions.question: What is our MTTR looking like? The VP of Eng wants it for the reliability review.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (resolved_at::TIMESTAMP - created_at::TIMESTAMP)) / 60.0), 1) AS answer FROM pagerduty.incidents WHERE status = 'resolved' AND resolved_at IS NOT NULL
questions.explanation: MTTR should only include resolved incidents (not triggered or acknowledged). Time diff in minutes.

questions.question: How reliable are our production deploys? I need the success rate for the SRE report.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'success' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 1) AS answer FROM github.deployments WHERE environment = 'production'
questions.explanation: Success rate = successful production deploys / total production deploys.

questions.question: Who is shipping the most code? Show me the top contributors by merged PRs.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT author, COUNT(*) AS merged_prs FROM github.pull_requests WHERE state = 'merged' GROUP BY author ORDER BY merged_prs DESC LIMIT 5
questions.expected_answer: ranked list of authors by merged PR count
questions.explanation: Must filter to merged (not just closed), then group and rank by author.

questions.question: Which services are the biggest oncall pain? I want to see where escalations keep happening.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT s.name AS service_name, COUNT(*) AS escalated_incidents FROM pagerduty.incidents i JOIN pagerduty.services s ON i.service_id = s.id WHERE i.escalation_count > 0 GROUP BY s.name ORDER BY escalated_incidents DESC LIMIT 5
questions.expected_answer: ranked list of services by escalated incidents
questions.explanation: Join incidents to services within PagerDuty, filter escalated, rank.

questions.question: Are our PRs getting too big? What is the average number of lines added per merged PR?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(additions), 1) AS answer FROM github.pull_requests WHERE state = 'merged'
questions.explanation: Average of additions field, filtered to only merged PRs.

questions.question: For each service on PagerDuty, how many failed deploys have there been? I want to see if bad deploys are driving our incidents.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT s.name AS service_name, COUNT(d.id) AS failed_deploys FROM pagerduty.services s LEFT JOIN github.deployments d ON s.name = d.repo_id AND d.status = 'failure' GROUP BY s.name ORDER BY failed_deploys DESC
questions.expected_answer: table of services with their failed deployment counts
questions.explanation: Cross-source join between PagerDuty services and GitHub deployments via service/repo name.

questions.question: Which services are in the worst shape right now — alerting in Datadog AND have unresolved PagerDuty incidents?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT DISTINCT dm.service_tag AS service_name FROM datadog.monitors dm JOIN pagerduty.services ps ON dm.service_tag = ps.name JOIN pagerduty.incidents pi ON ps.id = pi.service_id WHERE dm.status = 'Alert' AND pi.status IN ('triggered', 'acknowledged')
questions.expected_answer: list of services with both alerts and open incidents
questions.explanation: Cross-source join: Datadog monitors to PagerDuty services via service_tag/name, then to incidents.

questions.question: I have a theory that teams that deploy more have more incidents. Can you show me deploy frequency vs incident count by service?
questions.ground_truth_sql: WITH deploy_counts AS (SELECT repo_id AS service_name, COUNT(*) AS deploy_count FROM github.deployments WHERE environment = 'production' GROUP BY repo_id), incident_counts AS (SELECT s.name AS service_name, COUNT(*) AS incident_count FROM pagerduty.incidents i JOIN pagerduty.services s ON i.service_id = s.id GROUP BY s.name) SELECT dc.service_name, dc.deploy_count, COALESCE(ic.incident_count, 0) AS incident_count FROM deploy_counts dc LEFT JOIN incident_counts ic ON dc.service_name = ic.service_name ORDER BY dc.deploy_count DESC LIMIT 10
questions.expected_answer: table of services with deploy count and incident count
questions.explanation: Cross-source aggregation comparing GitHub deployment frequency with PagerDuty incident frequency per service.

questions.question: For our most problematic services — the ones with high-urgency incidents — what do their PR merge times and deploy success rates look like? I want to see if there is a process problem.
questions.ground_truth_sql: WITH high_urgency_services AS (SELECT DISTINCT s.name FROM pagerduty.incidents i JOIN pagerduty.services s ON i.service_id = s.id WHERE i.urgency = 'high'), pr_stats AS (SELECT repo_id AS service_name, AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (merged_at::TIMESTAMP - created_at::TIMESTAMP)) / 3600.0) AS avg_merge_hours FROM github.pull_requests WHERE state = 'merged' AND merged_at IS NOT NULL GROUP BY repo_id), deploy_stats AS (SELECT repo_id AS service_name, ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'success' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 1) AS success_rate FROM github.deployments WHERE environment = 'production' GROUP BY repo_id) SELECT hus.name AS service_name, ROUND(ps.avg_merge_hours, 1) AS avg_merge_hours, ds.success_rate FROM high_urgency_services hus LEFT JOIN pr_stats ps ON hus.name = ps.service_name LEFT JOIN deploy_stats ds ON hus.name = ds.service_name ORDER BY ds.success_rate ASC
questions.expected_answer: table of high-urgency services with merge time and deploy success rate
questions.explanation: Three-way cross-source analysis: PagerDuty for incident urgency, GitHub PRs for merge time, GitHub deployments for success rate.

questions.question: Which engineers are shipping the most code to services that keep having incidents? I need to know if specific people are introducing instability.
questions.ground_truth_sql: WITH deploy_services AS (SELECT DISTINCT d.repo_id AS service_name FROM github.deployments d WHERE d.environment = 'production'), incident_services AS (SELECT DISTINCT s.name AS service_name FROM pagerduty.incidents i JOIN pagerduty.services s ON i.service_id = s.id), problem_services AS (SELECT ds.service_name FROM deploy_services ds JOIN incident_services iss ON ds.service_name = iss.service_name) SELECT pr.author, COUNT(*) AS merged_prs_to_problem_services FROM github.pull_requests pr JOIN problem_services ps ON pr.repo_id = ps.service_name WHERE pr.state = 'merged' GROUP BY pr.author ORDER BY merged_prs_to_problem_services DESC LIMIT 5
questions.expected_answer: ranked list of PR authors by deploys to services with incidents
questions.explanation: Complex cross-source chain: GitHub PRs -> deployments -> PagerDuty services with incidents. Identifies who deploys most to problematic services.

customer_support.description: Customer support operations: Zendesk tickets + product analytics + Stripe billing

questions.question: Give me a health check on our support queue — how many tickets do we have total?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS answer FROM zendesk.tickets
questions.explanation: Simple count of all tickets.

questions.question: What is our solved ticket count? I need it for the support team dashboard.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS answer FROM zendesk.tickets WHERE status = 'solved'
questions.explanation: Simple filtered count. Note: solved != closed.

questions.question: Are users hitting a lot of errors in the product? I need the error event count for the bug triage.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS answer FROM analytics.events WHERE event_name = 'error'
questions.explanation: Simple filtered count on event type.

questions.question: I am prepping the enterprise QBR deck — what is our enterprise customer count?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS answer FROM stripe_support.customers WHERE plan = 'enterprise'
questions.explanation: Simple filtered count on plan type.

questions.question: Are there tickets sitting in the queue that nobody has touched yet?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS answer FROM zendesk.tickets WHERE status = 'new'
questions.explanation: Simple filtered count.

questions.question: How happy are our customers? I need the CSAT number for the QBR.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN satisfaction_rating = 'good' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / SUM(CASE WHEN satisfaction_rating IN ('good', 'bad') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 1) AS answer FROM zendesk.tickets
questions.explanation: CSAT = good / (good + bad), NOT good / total. Must exclude 'offered' and 'unoffered' from denominator.

questions.question: Who are our top support agents? I want to see who is crushing it on ticket resolution.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT a.name AS agent_name, COUNT(*) AS solved_count FROM zendesk.tickets t JOIN zendesk.agents a ON t.assignee_id = a.id WHERE t.status = 'solved' GROUP BY a.name ORDER BY solved_count DESC LIMIT 5
questions.expected_answer: ranked list of agents by solved tickets
questions.explanation: Join tickets to agents within Zendesk, filter solved, rank.

questions.question: I need to segment our high-value accounts — which customers are paying more than $99 a month?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS answer FROM stripe_support.customers WHERE mrr_cents > 9900
questions.explanation: $99 = 9900 cents. Must compare against cents value, not dollar value.

questions.question: How fast are we solving tickets on average? I need to know if our SLA is healthy.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (solved_at::TIMESTAMP - created_at::TIMESTAMP)) / 3600.0), 1) AS answer FROM zendesk.tickets WHERE status = 'solved' AND solved_at IS NOT NULL
questions.explanation: Time to solve only for solved tickets with a solved_at timestamp.

questions.question: Break down our ticket queue by priority — I want to see how many are urgent vs. low priority and what percentage each is.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT priority, COUNT(*) AS ticket_count, ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM zendesk.tickets), 1) AS percentage FROM zendesk.tickets GROUP BY priority ORDER BY ticket_count DESC
questions.expected_answer: table of priorities with counts and percentages
questions.explanation: Group by priority with both absolute and relative counts.

questions.question: Do our paying customers who file support tickets have higher or lower MRR than those who do not? I want to understand if support load correlates with account value.
questions.ground_truth_sql: WITH ticket_customers AS (SELECT DISTINCT requester_email FROM zendesk.tickets) SELECT CASE WHEN tc.requester_email IS NOT NULL THEN 'Has tickets' ELSE 'No tickets' END AS segment, ROUND(AVG(sc.mrr_cents) / 100.0, 2) AS avg_mrr_dollars FROM stripe_support.customers sc LEFT JOIN ticket_customers tc ON sc.email = tc.requester_email GROUP BY segment
questions.expected_answer: comparison table of MRR for ticket vs no-ticket customers
questions.explanation: Cross-source join via email between Zendesk tickets and Stripe customers. MRR in cents must be converted.

questions.question: Our enterprise customers are complaining about bugs — which ones are actually seeing the most errors in the product?
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT sc.name AS customer_name, sc.email, COUNT(ae.id) AS error_count FROM stripe_support.customers sc JOIN analytics.events ae ON sc.email = ae.user_email WHERE sc.plan = 'enterprise' AND ae.event_name = 'error' GROUP BY sc.name, sc.email ORDER BY error_count DESC LIMIT 10
questions.expected_answer: ranked list of enterprise customers by error count
questions.explanation: Cross-source join: Stripe customers filtered to enterprise, joined to analytics events filtered to errors.

questions.question: How much MRR is at risk from unhappy customers? I need to know the revenue exposure from bad CSAT ratings.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT ROUND(SUM(DISTINCT sc.mrr_cents) / 100.0, 2) AS mrr_at_risk_dollars FROM zendesk.tickets t JOIN stripe_support.customers sc ON t.requester_email = sc.email WHERE t.satisfaction_rating = 'bad'
questions.explanation: Cross-source join to find MRR of unhappy customers. Must use DISTINCT to avoid double-counting customers with multiple bad tickets.

questions.question: I have a hunch that our power users file more support tickets. Can you compare product usage with ticket volume for our most active users?
questions.ground_truth_sql: WITH event_counts AS (SELECT user_email, COUNT(*) AS event_count FROM analytics.events GROUP BY user_email ORDER BY event_count DESC LIMIT 10), ticket_counts AS (SELECT requester_email, COUNT(*) AS ticket_count FROM zendesk.tickets GROUP BY requester_email) SELECT ec.user_email, ec.event_count, COALESCE(tc.ticket_count, 0) AS ticket_count FROM event_counts ec LEFT JOIN ticket_counts tc ON ec.user_email = tc.requester_email ORDER BY ec.event_count DESC
questions.expected_answer: table comparing product usage with support ticket volume
questions.explanation: Cross-source analysis: analytics events for product usage, Zendesk for ticket volume, joined by email.

questions.question: Does customer satisfaction vary by pricing tier? I want to see if our free users are happier or angrier than enterprise — break down CSAT by plan.
questions.ground_truth_sql: SELECT sc.plan, ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN t.satisfaction_rating = 'good' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN t.satisfaction_rating IN ('good', 'bad') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0), 1) AS csat_percentage, COUNT(*) AS total_tickets FROM zendesk.tickets t JOIN stripe_support.customers sc ON t.requester_email = sc.email GROUP BY sc.plan ORDER BY csat_percentage DESC
questions.expected_answer: table of plans with their CSAT scores
questions.explanation: Cross-source join to segment CSAT by billing plan. Must use correct CSAT formula: good/(good+bad).
